evals.prompt: Which attribution model should we use?
evals.expected_output: Should not directly recommend a model. Should first ask 'what decision is the attribution model going to inform?' Should walk the what-question-are-you-answering framework: channel weighting, incremental lift, or holdout-validated causal truth. Should explain that different questions require different methodologies. Should NOT recommend a single model as universally correct.
assertions: Checks for .agents/modern-ai-context.md
Does NOT directly recommend a model first
Walks the what-question-are-you-answering framework
Distinguishes channel weighting from incremental lift from causal truth
Explains that different questions require different methodologies
Does not recommend a single model as universally correct
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections
Names a single highest-leverage next move

evals.prompt: Our last-touch attribution says Meta drives 35% of revenue. Should we double down?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis that 35% last-touch credit does not equal 35% incremental contribution. Should explain that the channel might be 90% redundant (customer would have converted anyway) or fully incremental. Should recommend an incrementality test (audience holdout in Meta or geo holdout) before doubling spend. Should also recommend comparing against first-touch attribution to see whether Meta is upper-funnel or closer.
assertions: Identifies that last-touch credit does not equal incremental contribution
Explains the redundancy possibility
Recommends an incrementality test before scaling
Names audience holdout or geo holdout as methodologies
Recommends first-touch vs last-touch comparison
Calls modern.attribution.first_touch and modern.attribution.last_touch
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: What's the difference between MMM and MTA?
evals.expected_output: Walks the MMM-vs-MTA-tradeoffs framework. MMM is aggregate, slower, durable to tracking loss, best for top-of-funnel and offline channels. MTA is granular, real-time, biased by tracking gaps, best for direct-response with clean tracking. Should recommend the hybrid: MTA for direct-response paid channels, MMM for top-of-funnel and offline, incrementality tests for triangulation. Should warn against MTA alone for tracking-weak channels.
assertions: Walks the MMM-vs-MTA framework with both approaches
Names MMM as aggregate, durable to tracking loss
Names MTA as granular, biased by tracking gaps
Recommends the hybrid approach
Warns against MTA alone for tracking-weak channels
Names incrementality tests as triangulation
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Our Meta ROAS has been dropping since iOS-14. Is the platform broken or are we?
evals.expected_output: Walks the post-iOS-14-reality framework. Should explain that iOS 14 permanently degraded clickstream-based attribution; reported ROAS dropped because signal completeness dropped, not necessarily because actual performance dropped. Should recommend server-side tracking (CAPI), Enhanced Conversions, first-party data investment, and triangulation with MMM or incrementality tests. Should warn that 2019-era assumptions about deterministic last-touch attribution are systematically misallocating budget.
assertions: Walks the post-iOS-14-reality framework
Explains the signal-degradation cause of the ROAS drop
Recommends server-side tracking (CAPI)
Recommends Enhanced Conversions or modeled conversions
Recommends first-party data investment
Recommends triangulation with MMM or incrementality tests
Calls modern.ads.roas via modern-mcp
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: How do I actually run an incrementality test on Meta?
evals.expected_output: Walks the incrementality-testing framework with the three approaches. Should recommend audience holdout in Meta as the most accessible (Meta supports random audience exclusion). Should explain the design: random subset excluded from campaign, measure conversion difference. Should recommend running at least once per quarter on the top channels. Should reference ab-testing for the underlying methodology.
assertions: Walks the incrementality-testing framework
Recommends audience holdout in Meta as accessible methodology
Explains the design (random subset excluded, measure difference)
Recommends at least quarterly cadence on top channels
References ab-testing for the underlying methodology
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We've been using the same attribution model for two years. When should we update it?
evals.expected_output: Walks the revisit-cadence framework. Should name the triggering conditions: channel mix shift of 20%+, ad-platform attribution methodology changes, tracking environment changes (privacy regs, iOS/Android updates), strategic-question shifts, and the quarterly minimum. Should warn that set-and-forget attribution leads to misalignment and that budget-allocation decisions go badly when the model is stale.
assertions: Walks the revisit-cadence framework
Names channel mix shift threshold (20%+)
Names ad-platform changes as triggers
Names tracking environment changes as triggers
Names quarterly minimum review
Warns that set-and-forget leads to misallocation
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: What's wrong with just using last-touch attribution? It's simple and the platform supports it.
evals.expected_output: Walks the attribution-model-taxonomy and your-attribution-is-wrong-embrace-the-directional-truth frameworks. Should explain that last-touch is biased toward direct, branded search, and retargeting (channels that capture the final touch). It systematically underweights upper-funnel demand creators. Should not advocate against it categorically but should explain the bias and recommend triangulation with first-touch (and incrementality where the decision matters). Should note all attribution is directionally wrong.
assertions: Walks the attribution-model-taxonomy framework
Explains last-touch's bias toward direct, branded, retargeting
Names that last-touch underweights upper-funnel demand creators
Does not advocate against last-touch categorically
Recommends triangulation with first-touch and incrementality
Notes all attribution is directionally wrong
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections
